channel.title: Epilog
channel.description: Updates for the Epilog macOS app

item.title: Epilog 0.17
item.description:
What's new

  • A rebuilt timer. The focus timer now runs on a rewritten engine — pause and resume land exactly where you left off, the "previous" button behaves predictably, and countdowns stay honest across every interval and break.
  • Grace time. When a session ends, you can add five more minutes without losing your place — and if you run extra-extra time we extend your entry duration.
  • Your reminder, your schedule. Pick the time of day and the specific weekdays you want to be nudged to write. Night owls are covered — evenings run late into the early morning.
  • Report a bug or share feedback, in-app. A new Report a Bug / Feature Request / Feedback option sends your note straight to us — no email client required.

Fixes

  • Offline edits no longer get clobbered. Entries written while you're offline now sync back field-by-field instead of overwriting the whole day, and a sync indicator shows when your data is safely saved. Epilog also recovers on its own from silent sync failures.
  • Smaller cleanups across navigation and the day views for a steadier, more consistent feel.

item.title: Epilog 0.16
item.description:
AI summaries, your choice of provider

  • Connect ChatGPT using your own OpenAI API key — alongside the existing Claude API support
  • Your AI preferences now follow your account, and API keys are kept separate per account
  • Summaries now speak in your own voice and stay shorter and more scannable

A cleaner Settings window

  • Settings reorganized into grouped sections, in the style of macOS System Settings
  • Full open-source license texts are now included

Fixes

  • Fixed the auto-updater failing to start when the app was in the foreground from launch
  • Fixed the summary progress spinner clipping in the toolbar
  • AI status rows no longer appear while AI services are turned off

item.title: Epilog 0.15
item.description:
Instantly Summarize Your Day using AI

  • Epilog can now write a professional summary of your day: a short overview plus a breakdown for each category, updated automatically as you add entries.
  • Summaries are generated on your Mac with Apple Intelligence (macOS 26) — your entries never leave the device.
  • Summary text is fully selectable and pastes as clean text into Slack, Notes, or anywhere else — ready for stand-ups.
  • A new Enable AI Services switch turns all of it off in one place.

Bring Your Own Claude

  • Prefer a cloud model? Add your Anthropic API key and summaries are generated by Claude instead — using any model available with your key, including effort levels.
  • Works on any Mac, including ones without Apple Intelligence. Entries are sent to Anthropic only when Claude is selected, and usage is billed to your key.

Timer

  • The Previous button on a break now restarts the break first; press it again to step back an interval.

Fixes and refinements

  • Window size and position are remembered as separate settings, so Today keeps its own window frame separate from My Days and Settings.
  • Fixed a rare issue where custom categories could be replaced by the starter set.
  • Smoother category menu show/hide animation in My Days.

item.title: Epilog 0.14
item.description:
Autocomplete for tags & mentions

  • Start typing # or @ in an entry and Epilog suggests your most-used tag right in the text — press Tab or → to accept it, ↑/↓ to browse other matches
  • Suggestions are scoped to the journal you're writing in, so each category offers its own tags

Tags, everywhere they belong

  • Tags from your past entries now show up reliably in tag clouds and filters — including older entries where they'd previously gone missing
  • Tags and mentions display in a softer tone that stays out of the way of your writing

Windows that remember

  • Each view — timer, My Days, Settings — keeps its own window size and position, and switching views glides the window into place

Account

  • Change your password anytime from Settings → My Account

item.title: Epilog 0.13
item.description:
Find Your #Tags & @Mentions

  • Every journal in the sidebar now shows a tag cloud for #tags and @mentions — now it's easier to discover what you've been writing about at a glance, and jump straight to any tag.
  • Tags now carry their journal's color throughout your days and entries.

Weeks that start on Monday

  • Weeks now follow the ISO standard — Monday through Sunday — consistently on every device and in every region.
  • Weeks around New Year's are labeled correctly: late-December days that belong to the new year's first week now say so.
  • Your journal reorganizes itself automatically the first time you launch this version — nothing for you to do.

item.title: Epilog 0.12
item.description:
Smoother, faster, and better through the night.

  • Overnight sessions get their due. Entries that cross midnight now show up on both days — finish at 1:30 AM and you'll find the entry under yesterday and this morning. Time labels adapt the way Messages does: "Yesterday 11:00 PM – Today 1:30 AM" today, "Fri, Jul 10 11:00 PM – Sat, Jul 11 1:00 AM" when you look back later.
  • Your category sticks. New entries now default to the last category you used — including after relaunching Epilog.
  • No more stutters. Fixed the hangs that could freeze the countdown, the menu bar timer, and scrolling. Epilog stays responsive even with years of journal history.
  • Steadier at launch. Fixed the most common crash — starting a second copy of Epilog while one was already running.
  • Sharper AI connections. Agents logging your work over MCP can now use precise timestamps, so late-night entries land on the right day.

item.title: Epilog 0.11
item.description:
Epilog 0.11

Deleting is no longer forever. Deleted entries — whether removed by you or by a connected AI app — are now recoverable for 30 days instead of being erased immediately. AI apps get a matching restore ability, and previous versions of edited entries are kept.

AI apps now work under guardrails. Every entry records who wrote it — you, or a specific AI app on a specific device. AI apps must prove they've read an entry before they can delete it, you decide how many entries one AI conversation may delete (Settings → AI Connections), and every AI change is logged on your Mac.

Smarter AI journaling. Connected AI apps can now log entries that cross midnight, add to an existing time block without creating duplicates, give each line its own category, review any span of your journal — a quarter, a year, all of it — and verify exactly which Epilog they're talking to.

Fixes

  • Overnight entries that end after midnight no longer show "(Yesterday)" beside a time that's already today.
  • Repeated #hashtags and @mentions in a single entry are no longer counted twice.
  • AI connections stay responsive while the app is busy, and simultaneous AI edits can no longer overwrite each other.

item.title: Epilog 0.10
item.description:
Connect your AI apps

Epilog can now act as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server, letting AI apps on your Mac work with your journal — Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and any other MCP-capable app. They can search and read your entries, log new ones for you, and write day and week summaries.

  • Turn it on in Settings → AI Connections, then copy the setup snippet for your AI app.
  • Off by default. Connections only work while Epilog is running and signed in, and you can turn them off anytime.
  • Once enabled, a status button appears in the toolbar: green while the server is running. Click it to toggle AI Connections without opening Settings.

User Guide (in progress)

Epilog now has a built-in User Guide. Mac users can now open it from the Help menu or press ⌘?. It includes step-by-step AI Connections setup and a full command reference. More topics are on the way.

item.title: Epilog 0.9
item.description:
What's New

  • Entry Editor: press Up button to reuse prior entries
  • Epilog on macOS can now look for and install updates — on by default, opt out in Settings → General
  • A Download button will show when updates are available
  • Sign in with Apple or Google added

Bug Fixes

  • Tint colors
  • Various layouts
  • Various fixes in the iPhone & iPad apps
